pearls.text: The mesorectal fascia propria is the key anatomical structure in TME. Intact mesorectum correlates with <5% local recurrence at 5 years versus >20% with mesorectal defects.
R0 resection requires tumour-free margins. The minimum acceptable margin width remains debated: 1mm is widely accepted for colorectal, but 5mm or more is preferred where anatomy permits.
The FINER criteria define a good research question: Feasible, Interesting, Novel, Ethical, Relevant. Every study must pass all five before proceeding.
For cT3 N+ rectal cancer with threatened CRM, NCCN v2.2024 mandates long-course chemoradiotherapy (45 Gy + capecitabine) before surgery — not short-course RT.
Complete Mesocolic Excision (CME) for colon cancer follows the same embryological plane logic as TME for rectum: dissect in the correct fascial plane to remove an intact mesocolon.
The IMRaD structure (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion) is not arbitrary — each section answers a specific question: Why? How? What? So what?

Sentinel node biopsy is the standard axillary staging for clinically node-negative breast cancer. The ACOSOG Z0011 trial established that completion axillary dissection can be omitted when 1–2 sentinel nodes are positive in selected patients.
The Couinaud classification divides the liver into 8 functionally independent segments based on portal pedicles and hepatic veins — each can be resected independently.
In gastric cancer, D2 lymphadenectomy is the standard for curative-intent surgery. The 15-year Dutch D1D2 trial follow-up confirmed a survival advantage for D2 over D1.

CONSORT item 12a requires reporting of statistical methods used to compare groups. Simply stating 'Student t-test was used' is insufficient — report the test, its assumptions, and whether they were verified.
Null hypothesis (H₀) states there is no difference between groups. The study is designed to reject H₀, not to prove the alternative — a critical distinction in interpreting p-values.
Anastomotic leak after colorectal surgery is graded by the International Study Group definition: Grade A (no intervention), Grade B (intervention without re-laparotomy), Grade C (re-laparotomy).
The multidisciplinary tumour board (MDT) is not optional — evidence shows MDT review changes management in 20–30% of cases and is independently associated with improved oncological outcomes.
Peritoneal metastasis from colorectal cancer is now classified as M1c (AJCC 8th), acknowledging it has a worse prognosis than isolated hepatic or pulmonary metastasis.
HARKing — Hypothesising After Results are Known — is a form of research misconduct that inflates type I error rates and should be explicitly avoided in study planning.
The STROBE checklist (Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology) has 22 items applicable to cohort, case-control, and cross-sectional studies.
For retroperitoneal sarcoma, the European approach (TARPSWG guidelines) recommends extended resection including adherent organs en bloc, accepting higher morbidity to achieve R0.
The CRS+HIPEC procedure for peritoneal carcinomatosis from colorectal origin: Sugarbaker's landmark RCT (2003) showed median OS 22.3 vs 12.6 months for HIPEC vs systemic chemotherapy alone.
The Roux-en-Y gastric bypass creates a 15–30 mL gastric pouch with a 100–150 cm alimentary limb. Weight loss is driven by restriction AND metabolic hormone changes (GLP-1, ghrelin reduction).
In thyroid surgery, the recurrent laryngeal nerve (RLN) must be identified and traced for its entire course — not just avoided. Identification reduces injury risk from 2% to <0.5%.
